1→# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
     2→# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
     3→#
     4→# Usage:
     5→#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
     6→#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
     7→#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
     8→
     9→TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
    10→PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
    11→
    12→CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    13→AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    14→LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    15→
    16→ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
    17→CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
    18→ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
    19→LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
    20→
    21→QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
    22→QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
    23→
    24→SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
    25→NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
    26→
    27→# Assembly sources — single-precision
    28→ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
    29→           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
    30→           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
    31→           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
    32→           sf_fpclassify.s
    33→ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
    34→
    35→# Assembly sources — double-precision
    36→S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
    37→             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
    38→             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
    39→             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
    40→             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
    41→             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
    42→S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
    43→
    44→# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
    45→# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.
    46→
    47→# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
    48→FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
    49→               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
    50→               modff fpclassifyf
    51→
    52→# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
    53→FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
    54→                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
    55→                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo
    56→
    57→# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
    58→FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
    59→                     hypot remainder lrint lround
    60→
    61→# All functions to test
    62→FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)
    63→
    64→# Test output directory
    65→TESTDIR = test
    66→
    67→# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
    68→V ?= 0
    69→ifeq ($(V),1)
    70→  Q =
    71→  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
    72→  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
    73→  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
    74→else
    75→  Q = @
    76→  MSG_AS =
    77→  MSG_CC =
    78→  MSG_LD =
    79→endif
    80→
    81→# --- Pattern rules ---
    82→
    83→%.o: %.s
    84→	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
    85→
    86→$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
    87→	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
    88→
    89→# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
    90→# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
    91→# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
    92→define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
    93→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
    94→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
    95→
    96→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
    97→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
    98→
    99→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   100→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   101→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
   102→	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   103→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   104→	else \
   105→		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
   106→			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
   107→	fi
   108→endef
   109→
   110→# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
   111→define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
   112→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
   113→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   114→
   115→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   116→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   117→
   118→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   119→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   120→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
   121→	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   122→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   123→	else \
   124→		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
   125→			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
   126→	fi
   127→endef
   128→
   129→# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
   130→define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
   131→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   132→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   133→
   134→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   135→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   136→	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
   137→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   138→	else \
   139→		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
   140→	fi
   141→endef
   142→
   143→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
   144→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
   145→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))
   146→
   147→# --- Top-level targets ---
   148→
   149→OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
   150→           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
   151→REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)
   152→
   153→all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)
   154→
   155→test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
   156→	@echo "Done."
   157→
   158→size: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
   159→	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
   160→	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
   161→		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
   162→			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
   163→	@echo "  --------------------"
   164→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
   165→		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')
   166→
   167→list:
   168→	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
   169→	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"
   170→
   171→clean:
   172→	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt
   173→
   174→.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
   175→

file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

content.text:
Files in `test/` not referenced by any `FUNCS_*` list in the Makefile:
- `test_asinhf.c`
- `test_cbrtf.c`
- `test_j0.c`, `test_j1.c`, `test_jn.c`
- `test_y0.c`, `test_y1.c`, `test_yn.c`

input.description: Delete 8 unused test C files not in Makefile FUNCS lists
